Siad's body was discovered on Monday evening. An autopsy will be carried out ​to ‌determine the cause of death, the Nanterre prosecutor's ⁠office said. Siad's name appeared nearly 2,000 times in the Epstein files released by the ‌U.S. Department of Justice. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

At least 50 soldiers were killed in an ambush near the northern town of Anefis, in an attack claimed by Tuareg separatists and Al-Qaeda-linked jihadists. The assault is the latest in a string of setbacks, with militants also targeting Malian and Russian Africa Corps forces, downing a military helicopter, and briefly seizing territory. The UN cultural agency is set to male a decision between July 24 and 27.

items.description: The craze for Argentina has endured for decades in Bangladesh, a country of 170 million people who have never qualified for the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Louvre's Apollo Gallery reopens, 9 months after heist
items.description: The 17th-century Apollo Gallery reopened to the public without precious jewels or other objects previously on display.

items.title: Kenyan lawyers launch nationwide strike over judicial corruption and delays
items.description: Kenyan lawyers staged a nationwide boycott of court proceedings on Wednesday to protest judicial corruption and chronic delays.

items.title: India: Youth-led 'Cockroach' protests increase pressure on Modi's government
items.description: With each passing day, India's youth-led cockroach movement seems to swell. As the sit-in by anti-corruption campaigners grows in New Delhi, opposition lawmakers relay their call for the education minister to resign over the leaking of exam tests. The standoff grew with the Supreme Court refusing to hear a police brutality petition launched after Monday's crackdown on those trying to reach parliament. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondent in New Delhi, Sarah Jacob.

items.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
items.description: Daniel Siad, whose name appears in the Epstein files thousands of times, denied knowledge of the threat Jeffrey Epstein posed.

items.title: Argentina's press laments Albiceleste defeat, hails end of an era
items.description: Press reflects on Messi's emotional farewell and an uncertain future; Headlines focus on tears, farewell fears and the end of an era. Leer más

items.title: Cairo's historic Sultan Qaytbay Mosque-Mausoleum reopens
items.description: The mosque-mausoleum of the ruler of the Mamluk Sultanate has reopened after years of restoration in Cairo.

items.title: Trump set to expand power cost pledge on data centers

U.S. announces civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia

items.title: Judge hearing evidence against singer D4vd in killing, dismemberment of teen
items.description: Prosecutors on Tuesday showed a body camera video with singer D4vd telling sheriff's deputies who were investigating a missing person's report that he had only met then-13-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez once in person, and he did not know she was a minor.

items.title: LASTMA arrests tanker driver after dispatch rider died in Lagos
items.description: The agency explained that the driver fled the scene after the crash without assisting the victim or reporting the incident to the authorities. The post LASTMA arrests tanker driver after dispatch rider died in Lagos appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: UN chief Guterres to visit Cyprus in bid to revive peace talks
items.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

items.title: Mysterious white foam draws crowds on  Nigerian highway
items.description: The environmental agency has warned people to avoid going near the substance as it could pose a health risk.

items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires top military commander amid ongoing protests
items.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fires the country's army chief, just days after he dismissed the defense minister. Those two key figures had been feuding with each other.

items.title: Trump to attend dignified transfer of fallen soldiers. And, Hegseth testifies on Iran
items.description: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.

items.title: UNAIDS Chief urges Africa to rethink health funding after global aid cuts
items.description: The head of UNAIDS is calling for what she describes as a "radical change" in how Africa funds healthcare, following deep cuts to international aid.

items.description: Anthony Albanese will announce an expansion of the Commonwealth's "paid prac" scheme at Labor's National Conference. Follow live.

items.title: Neuquén Province to pitch first US bond since 2017
items.description: Neuquén province will pitch foreign investors in New York on its first new international bond in nearly a decade as the local government reaps the rewards of a shale oil boom. Leer más

items.title: France: Minister quits over pesticide bill — but is staying
items.description: Environment Minister Monique Barbut submitted her resignation in protest over the reintroduction of pesticides that scientists say harm human health as well as bees. But President Emmanuel Macron refused to accept it.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: 2,000 volunteers join a painstaking effort to restore massive skeleton of prehistoric predator
items.description: Mastodonsaurus lived about 240 million years ago during the Middle Triassic period. It was an underwater ambush hunter, preying mainly on fish and smaller amphibians

items.title: Volunteers refuse to let cemetery vandals have final word

items.title: From hotel bedsheet to World Cup pitch: the story of Argentina’s Malvinas flag
items.description: Daubed with ink on a hotel bedsheet, the Malvinas banner became one of the symbols of Argentina's dramatic World Cup win over England. Its creator, who spoke exclusively to Perfil and asked to remain anonymous, explained how the flag came about, why the squad won't give it back and why he'll be at Sunday's final. Leer más

items.title: Cameroon: President Paul Biya’s prolonged absence raises questions
items.description: Described as one of the longest-serving presidents in Africa, Cameroonian President Paul Biya's absence from the country continues to draw criticism.

items.title: France recorded 5,700 more deaths than usual during its historic June heat wave
items.description: France recorded at least 5,700 more deaths than usual during a historic heatwave last month, the public health agency said Wednesday, sharply increasing its estimates of the feared toll from record-shattering temperatures. FRANCE 24's James Vasina reports.

items.description: FDA has questioned the evidence as demand surges for the compounds promoted for sleep, muscle growth, and anti-ageing.

items.title: Rebel Wilson's victory in defamation case could be short-lived. Here's why
items.description: As lawyers for actor Charlotte MacInnes plan to appeal a failed defamation case outcome against Hollywood star Rebel Wilson, a solicitor says the judge's interpretations could be grounds for a retrial or a different verdict.

items.title: New York mayor says city can't arrest Netanyahu on ICC warrant
items.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said Tuesday his office does not have the authority to carry out the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and called on US federal officials to step in
